description:
pyDREAM MonoSQ Normalized (Stoichiometry Compensator, Experimental)

Purpose:
Testing-stage MonoSQ variant for Pb/I workflows where selected single-atom component traces are used as stoichiometry compensators instead of ordinary sampled mixture weights. Existing MonoSQ templates are unchanged.

Structure Factor:
Monodisperse hard-sphere structure factor evaluated with calc_monodisperse_sq using the Percus-Yevick approximation. eff_r controls the effective hard-sphere radius and vol_frac controls the packing term.

Form Factor:
The model begins with the ordinary weighted mixture of MD-derived SAXS component profiles, but any component selected as a stoichiometry compensator is recomputed from the non-compensator weights before model evaluation. For Pb/I systems this allows a single-atom I- trace to supply the iodine needed to meet a configured target ratio while behaving mostly like a flat background contribution.

Model Equation:
Given non-compensator weights w_i, the template solves non-negative compensator weights c_j such that the component stoichiometry totals best match the configured target ratio. It then evaluates:
I_raw(q) = sum_i w_i I_i(q) S_HS(q; eff_r, vol_frac) + sum_j c_j I_j(q) S_HS(q; eff_r, vol_frac) + solv_w * I_solv(q)
I_model(q) = scale * I_raw(q) + offset

Likelihood Convention:
The pyDREAM likelihood compares the unmodified experimental intensity values directly against I_model(q), with a point-normalized Gaussian log-likelihood. scale and offset are applied only inside the forward model.

Template-Specific Setup:
The Prefit tab exposes a Stoichiometry Compensator section for this template. Configure target elements and ratio, then choose the weight parameters that should be recomputed as compensators. The Auto Guess action selects single-atom target-element components, which is intended to pick I-only traces when present.

Model Parameters:
w1, w2, ..., wN: Relative contribution of each MD-derived component profile. Selected compensator weights are recomputed by the model and should remain fixed in DREAM.
solv_w: Solvent-background multiplier applied to the solvent trace before the model scale is applied using the user-configured Prefit/DREAM bounds.
offset: Additive model baseline applied after the model scale.
eff_r: Effective hard-sphere radius used in the structure-factor calculation.
vol_frac: Hard-sphere packing fraction used in the Percus-Yevick structure factor.
scale: Multiplicative factor applied to the raw model curve.
